compiled_state.title: [ticket-platform-compat tests / alpine-busybox]: helper functions undefined under busybox sh -> 'clone_ticket_repo: command not found', 'assert_eq: command not found'
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Sub-Agent Blocker (surfaced by 9cb3-6ef0 fix; previously hidden by workflow startup_failure)

#### Expected Behavior

`bash tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh` should pass on Alpine 3.19 with busybox sh as the default shell (matrix leg `alpine-busybox`).

#### Actual Behavior

Run https://github.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra/actions/runs/25027977388/job/73303271819 produces uniform `command not found` errors. Helper functions defined in test setup are not in scope when individual test cases execute:

```
tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh: line 37: clone_ticket_repo: command not found
tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh: line 477: cd: /tmp/tmp.KcpJgj/repo: No such file or directory
tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh: line 480: assert_eq: command not found
```

`assert_eq` (from `tests/lib/assert.sh`) and `clone_ticket_repo` (test-local helper) are both undefined, suggesting the test file is being executed by busybox `sh` rather than `bash` despite the matrix entry's `shell: bash`. The workflow file does NOT explicitly set `shell: bash` on the `Run platform compat tests` step under the alpine container — the step `run: bash tests/scripts/...` invocation may be defeated by container-shell interpretation.

Final exit: `Process completed with exit code 127` (command not found is exit 127).

### 1. Technical Environment

- Workflow: `.github/workflows/ticket-platform-matrix.yml` matrix leg `alpine-busybox` (`ubuntu-latest`, container `alpine:3.19`, `shell: sh`)
- Tests file: `tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh`
- Surfaced on: PR #10 (https://github.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra/pull/10)

### 4. Chronological Rationalization

The matrix entry sets `shell: sh` (busybox sh), but the test step `run: bash tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh` should invoke bash explicitly. However, the alpine:3.19 container does not have bash installed by default — only the prior `Install dependencies (Alpine only)` step runs `apk add --no-cache bash python3 git`. Possibilities: (a) the conditional install step failed silently, (b) the install ran but PATH/which-bash resolves to busybox, (c) the test script's shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bash` is being overridden by the GA runner shell wrapper.

### 6. Logs

Excerpt from job 73303271819 (full log via `gh run view --job 73303271819 --log-failed`). Errors begin at line 37 (the location of `clone_ticket_repo` invocation in test setup) and continue uniformly through C4–C17.

### Suggested Investigation

1. Add a debug step before `Run platform compat tests`: `which bash; bash --version; echo "$SHELL"` to confirm bash is installed and visible.
2. Check if busybox `ash` is interpreting the test script despite the bash shebang. Try invoking with `/bin/bash tests/scripts/test-ticket-platform-compat.sh` explicitly.
3. Verify `apk add bash` succeeded in the prior step by inspecting that step's log.
